title: Policy Support for Developing Low-Cost Salmonella Test Kits for SMEs
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     illness     caused     by     Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of   gastroenteritis   worldwide  ,   resulting   in   significant     public     health     and     economic     burdens  .   Reliable     detection     of     Salmonella   in   food     requires     robust     laboratory     systems     with     consistent     quality     assurance   (QA)   programs  .   However  ,   many   testing   facilities     face     challenges     such   as   inadequate     standardization  ,   limited     proficiency   testing,   and     insufficient     validation     of     methods  ,   leading     to     variable     results  .   Strengthening   QA   systems     ensures     that     laboratories     provide     accurate  ,   reproducible  ,   and     timely   data   for     risk-based     decision  -making.   Implementing     internationally     recognized     standards  ,   such   as ISO 17025,   enhances     credibility     and     facilitates     trade     compliance  . Investments in QA   frameworks     build     public     trust  ,   support     effective     outbreak     response  ,   and     ultimately     reduce     the     incidence     of     Salmonella  -related     foodborne     illness     across     the     entire     food     supply     chain  .
